mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, aggregate consumer spending growth decelerates sharply, compressing corporate profit margins and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated household financial assets and rising consumer prices indicates strong pent-up demand translating into immediate corporate revenue growth, producing positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth decouples from CPIAUCSL acceleration, yielding a negative Rank IC over 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of supply-constrained expansion, rapid increases in industrial production signal successful bottleneck resolution and capacity utilization gains, triggering immediate positive market repricing.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in less than 45% of months where INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.35 million units annually indicate robust construction sector activity and wealth effects that spill over into broader equity markets, generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months where HOUST sustains levels above 1350.0 for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized growth in total nonfarm payrolls and real GDP confirms broad-based economic expansion, validating corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power when payroll growth outpaces GDP growth by more than 1.5 standard deviations, resulting in negative forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and forces market participants to price in tighter monetary policy, mechanically depressing forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of months where CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%, invalidating the inflation penalty mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: When policy rates remain near zero, elevated household financial assets act as a direct liquidity conduit into equity markets, sustaining positive forward returns through portfolio rebalancing flows.
failure_condition: The mechanism breaks down when RSAFS declines while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25%, producing negative 60-day returns in more than 70% of cases.
